title: Shuttle fiber frontmatter block
description: The shuttle: YAML block written and validated by the shuttle CLI. Daemon-owned fields are documented but not written by the CLI.

enabled.description: The dispatch signal. CLI sets true on install, false on uninstall. Daemon flips to false after successful oneshot run.

kind.description: Dispatch mode. oneshot: dispatched once (daemon clears enabled after run). standing: recurring per schedule.

interactive.description: When true, the dispatcher renders the Interactive Mode prompt block and the worker leaves the fiber active and the agent alive after its initial autonomous task. Absent or false means autonomous dispatch.

host.description: Machine id (own_host_id) of the daemon that owns dispatch for this fiber. The dispatch predicate is strict: a daemon dispatches a block iff block.host == its own_host_id. There is no nil-wildcard and no 'local' default — an absent/empty host is unowned and ineligible on every daemon. install/repeat stamp this by default; required when enabled=true.

project_dir.description: Absolute or tilde-prefixed working directory on the target host where the worker tmux session should start.

agent.description: Agent ID from the agent registry. Defaults to the registry's default agent when absent.

schedule.description: Present when kind=standing. Defines the recurrence.

expr.description: Standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour dom month dow). Example: '0 9 * * 1-5'.

review.description: Legacy per-run review state for standing roles. The authoritative home is now the per-host daemon runtime store (~/.shuttle/runtime.db); this frontmatter block is read-only compatibility for fibers dispatched before the eviction and is no longer written by the CLI or daemon. Runtime-only states such as due/running are runtime-store phases, never frontmatter.

state.description: Canonical persisted review state. scheduled means dispatchable when next_due_at is due; awaiting means a completed run needs human review; accepted records the most recently accepted run before the role returns to scheduled.

run_id.description: Current run identifier. Set by daemon when dispatching; ad-hoc standing runs use an adhoc- prefix.

completed_at.description: Completion timestamp for the run awaiting review. Set by daemon or worker handoff when review.state becomes awaiting.

accepted_run_id.description: Run id of the last accepted run. Set by CLI accept.

next_due_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime. Daemon-owned for standing roles after the first install; CLI sets initial value on repeat and advances on scheduled-run accept. Ad-hoc run accept preserves it.

last_run_at.description: Daemon-owned. Set when a run completes.

notes.writer_ownership: Document fields the CLI writes to synced frontmatter: enabled, kind, interactive, agent, schedule, host, project_dir. Runtime fields (review.state/run_id/completed_at/accepted_run_id, session, next_due_at, last_run_at) are NOT written to frontmatter anymore — they live in the per-host daemon runtime store (~/.shuttle/runtime.db), which is authoritative. CLI lifecycle verbs (accept/resume/pause/session-set) mutate that store via the daemon HTTP routes and fail closed when the daemon is unreachable rather than rewriting the synced fiber. The review/next_due_at/last_run_at blocks here remain only to validate and read legacy fibers. Constitution: shuttle-durable-runtime-store.
notes.derived_states: due and running are runtime-store phases derived from next_due_at and live worker state. They are not persisted review.state values.
notes.legacy_review_aliases: Older fibers may carry review.state=review or in_review for awaiting. Readers should normalize them when possible; new writers must use awaiting.
notes.schema_tz_migration: Pre-CLI standing roles used schedule.timezone. Migration renames to schedule.tz. The Elixir daemon reads tz after migration.
notes.kind_migration: Pre-CLI standing roles used shuttle.mode instead of shuttle.kind. Migration renames to kind.
